messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: That is interesting. IMO, I don't think it is needed since repeater and room nodes would technically not be moving hence the location would be static.
messages.content_html: That is interesting. IMO, I don't think it is needed since repeater and room nodes would technically not be moving hence the location would be static.

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: well, yes. but... manually added nodes eventually age-out unless updated. And many people don't know to A) manually add them, or B) manually update them. so, a single node on an established mesh network, can keep all of them updated and fresh.
messages.content_html: well, yes. but... manually added nodes eventually age-out unless updated. And many people don't know to A) manually add them, or B) manually update them. so, a single node on an established mesh network, can keep all of them updated and fresh.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: Understood. Also, from what I see on the map you can only remove or modify the node that You uploaded. Might make some obstacles for you. Only trial and error will tell us the true effect. Let me know how it goes.
messages.content_html: Understood. Also, from what I see on the map you can only remove or modify the node that You uploaded. Might make some obstacles for you. Only trial and error will tell us the true effect. Let me know how it goes.
messages.reply_html:

It's not so much about them moving, but more about them existing

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: @TF-Mambo recrof just confirmed, the script can update repeaters and room servers, as long as it gets a valid signature from the mesh adverts. you can't update companion locations that you didn't create, but the utility doesn't upload companions anyway.
messages.content_html: @TF-Mambo recrof just confirmed, the script can update repeaters and room servers, as long as it gets a valid signature from the mesh adverts. you can't update companion locations that you didn't create, but the utility doesn't upload companions anyway.

messages.content_html: Interesting. Thanks for that info. I am definitely curious to see what more projects come out for MeshCore.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: That's awesome. Its going to be a pretty big lift. Hopefully it works out how you want it.
messages.content_html: That's awesome. Its going to be a pretty big lift. Hopefully it works out how you want it.

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: I dunno. I'm hoping I'm not naive (and I very well could be) but the script used the meshtastic CLI to send the announcements.. meshcore has a CLI and supposedly it can send to a room node. So, assuming I can figure out the commands to actually send a message, I'd think it's just a matter of changing the meshtastic commands to the meshcore commands. Maybe even make the script configurable so people can use it for either (or both) meshes. Set some params at the top for things like which CLI to use, the channel name (for meshtastic) or channel/room name for meshcore, and if meshcore.. an optional room credential with read/write privs.
messages.content_html: I dunno. I'm hoping I'm not naive (and I very well could be) but the script used the meshtastic CLI to send the announcements.. meshcore has a CLI and supposedly it can send to a room node. So, assuming I can figure out the commands to actually send a message, I'd think it's just a matter of changing the meshtastic commands to the meshcore commands. Maybe even make the script configurable so people can use it for either (or both) meshes. Set some params at the top for things like which CLI to use, the channel name (for meshtastic) or channel/room name for meshcore, and if meshcore.. an optional room credential with read/write privs.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: Messaging to bump to the top. Latest update from me. I have the roof node on a G2 setup as repeater. I have an EDC companion with a WisMesh Tag. Also, setup a room node on a T-Beam. Additionally, I just setup a V3 as USB companion and have it linked through Home Assistant as a monitor and announcer to me directly that does not use the internet. SHTF mindset.
messages.content_html: Messaging to bump to the top. Latest update from me. I have the roof node on a G2 setup as repeater. I have an EDC companion with a WisMesh Tag. Also, setup a room node on a T-Beam. Additionally, I just setup a V3 as USB companion and have it linked through Home Assistant as a monitor and announcer to me directly that does not use the internet. SHTF mindset.

messages.author: RaceBannon
messages.text: Morning gang. New to Meshcore. I’m in Jacksonville. In the process of putting together repeater and two companion devices. I have 3 Heltec V3 that will be here tomorrow. Getting all the connectors tomorrow.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: Fantastic. The main rule of thumb higher is better. Put the antenna as high as you can possibly manage to get it. That is what will ultimately help us all reach and link distant repeaters.
messages.content_html: Fantastic. The main rule of thumb higher is better. Put the antenna as high as you can possibly manage to get it. That is what will ultimately help us all reach and link distant repeaters.

messages.author: Venice FL-Ken
messages.text: Thanks for the info and link. Currently dealing with a lot of rx noise, and not yet sure if it is in band or out of band. Lower gain antenna are providing better SNR. This is on the Meshtastic freq. Will have to monitor with SDR and try to determine the interference. Maybe it would not be present on the current Meshcore channel. I have not checked yet, as there is no meshcore activity within range. Hopefully that will change.

messages.text: Welcome. We are slowly building out the coverage. I believe you would be the first in Orange Park. We just have a member put a node up in Cape Coral yesterday. Please visit the site https://mc.flmesh.us and, if able to, setup an observer node and feed the Florida MC MQTT. This will help the map. Also, Once you have a repeater setup, you can wardrive and see your coverage.

messages.author: Venice FL-Ken
messages.text: @TF-Mambo I am setting up Meshcore nodes in Venice FL. I wonder if you could tell me what the noise floor reading is for your station G2 with cavity filter at your location. My Station G2 and Heltec V4 that both have LNA's show a noise floor of -106 to -100. Would this be useable as is, or would these units require a cavity filter to be useful in the mesh? Currently they are located about 22 feet above ground. Any thoughts you might have would be appreicated.
messages.content_html: @TF-Mambo I am setting up Meshcore nodes in Venice FL. I wonder if you could tell me what the noise floor reading is for your station G2 with cavity filter at your location. My Station G2 and Heltec V4 that both have LNA's show a noise floor of -106 to -100. Would this be useable as is, or would these units require a cavity filter to be useful in the mesh? Currently they are located about 22 feet above ground. Any thoughts you might have would be appreicated.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: My NF is averaging about -106. It has been as low as -118 and as high as -93, depending on weather and ducting.
messages.content_html: My NF is averaging about -106. It has been as low as -118 and as high as -93, depending on weather and ducting.
messages.reply_html:

Currently they are located about 22 feet above ground. Any thoughts you might have would be appreicated.

messages.author: Venice FL-Ken
messages.text: Build it and they will come I wonder what type of range/distance any of you are seeing with your nodes. Today i tried an experiment and took my Heltec V4 with a 8 3/4 in whip antenna to the canopy walk tower at Myakka River State Park, The top of the tower is about 74 feet above ground. The LOS view is great. But i could not get any repeaters and no response on public channel messages. My Venice repeater is about 14.3 miles away. Not much else in that Sarasota area. I was hoping that height would provide some results. Maybe if i try that again, I will try some different antennas. I do have a high gain yagi, but that kinda defeats the purpose for contributing to the mesh. Any best case results you can provide will give me a point of reference of what to expect. In any case it was a fun day at the park (edited)
messages.content_html: Build it and they will come I wonder what type of range/distance any of you are seeing with your nodes. Today i tried an experiment and took my Heltec V4 with a 8 3/4 in whip antenna to the canopy walk tower at Myakka River State Park, The top of the tower is about 74 feet above ground. The LOS view is great. But i could not get any repeaters and no response on public channel messages. My Venice repeater is about 14.3 miles away. Not much else in that Sarasota area. I was hoping that height would provide some results. Maybe if i try that again, I will try some different antennas. I do have a high gain yagi, but that kinda defeats the purpose for contributing to the mesh. Any best case results you can provide will give me a point of reference of what to expect. In any case it was a fun day at the park (edited)

messages.author: Neil
messages.text: Based on experience, we will need one more revision. The G3 is fully compatible with G2 firmware, and users can choose to upgrade to the Raspberry Pi Zero 2W
messages.content_html: Based on experience, we will need one more revision. The G3 is fully compatible with G2 firmware, and users can choose to upgrade to the Raspberry Pi Zero 2W
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: I'm curious on the specs compared to the G2. I have 4x G2s currently.
messages.content_html: I'm curious on the specs compared to the G2. I have 4x G2s currently.
messages.reply_html:
Neil
Based on experience, we will need one more revision. The G3 is fully compatible with G2 firmware, and users can choose to upgrade to the Raspberry Pi Zero 2W

messages.author: Neil
messages.text: There is no specification sheet yet. The PA has a slightly higher power rating and P1dB point. The total power consumption can be adjusted in several levels for further energy saving. The RX can control the external LNA gain for better adaptation to high-noise environments.
messages.content_html: There is no specification sheet yet. The PA has a slightly higher power rating and P1dB point. The total power consumption can be adjusted in several levels for further energy saving. The RX can control the external LNA gain for better adaptation to high-noise environments.

messages.author: TF-Mambo

messages.content_html: That sounds great. Thanks for working on the new design. The main issue I always see with the G2 is they can hear me but I can't hear them.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: You guys can change CR to 8 for now until we have more repeaters in the area. It will give more parity while sacrificing airtime. Once more nodes are in the area, you can dial it back.

Venice FL-Ken
Build it and they will come I wonder what type of range/distance any of you are seeing with your nodes. Today i tried an experiment and took my Heltec V4 with a 8 3/4 in whip antenna to the canopy walk tower at Myakka River State Park, The top of the tower is about 74 feet above ground. The LOS view is great. But i could not get any repeaters and no response on public channel messages. My Venice repeater is about 14.3 miles away. Not much else in that Sarasota area. I was hoping that height would provide some results. Maybe if i try that again, I will try some different antennas. I do have a high gain yagi, but that kinda defeats the purpose for contributing to the mesh. Any best case results you can provide will give me a point of reference of what to expect. In any case it was a fun day at the park (edited)

messages.author: Venice FL-Ken
messages.text: @TF-Mambo What firmware version are you using on your Station G2 ? I updated to version 1.13 and since that point, the noise figure is almost always reporting -120 ... and RX is very poor. I went back and re installed version 1.12 and now the NF is reading -90 to -100 ( i do not have any filters installed and antenna is pointing at water tower with cell phone antenna's, so probably explains the lower SNR readings. but my question is in regards to issue in updating to v 1.13 on the Station G2 ... any thoughts ? Also is there an easy way to turn off the SX1262 rx boost without compiling source code ? Ken

messages.author: Venice FL-Ken
messages.text: @TF-Mambo Thanks for the info... good to know about RX Boost ... Apparently something else is going on with my unit, since when i first reloaded v 1.12 I was getting the lower SNR as mentioned, i checked quite a few times, but then after just letting the unit run normally for some time, I went back and checked the NF of the repeater and it was back to -120 .... I do have the agc.reset.interval set to 4. but have also had it at 0 and other settings ... The repeater is outside over the roofline and my other companion and room server are inside and their tx power levels are set to zero ... and they are showing strong signals as receivied by the repeater, even with antennas horizontal ... So not sure what is going on .... Thanks again for the info on your setup ... it helps to hear about someone elses setup. Ken

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: You can always erase reflash to get it cleared. I have mine with the following commands: 'set tx 16' 'set multi.acks 1' 'set agc.reset.interval 12'
messages.content_html: You can always erase reflash to get it cleared. I have mine with the following commands: 'set tx 16' 'set multi.acks 1' 'set agc.reset.interval 12'

messages.author: Venice FL-Ken
messages.text: @TF-Mambo After doing more research, apparently the NF going to -120 and staying is a known issue with a problem with the agc.reset.interval implementation. https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1743 There seems to be a fix that is going to be included in a future release.
messages.content_html: @TF-Mambo After doing more research, apparently the NF going to -120 and staying is a known issue with a problem with the agc.reset.interval implementation. https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1743 There seems to be a fix that is going to be included in a future release.

messages.author: elvisimprsntr
messages.text: so far MT has been a huge dissapointment for me. Everyone is using medium-fast in SF (which is not the recommended default and deviates from the rest of Florida), which doesn't work for me becase i am in the first fresnel zone of another building 100 feet away, even with an outdoor solar node at 45' elevation. i can hear 250+ medium-fast nodes but can't communicate with any of them unless i am on top of an intercostal bridge or the jupiter lighthouse. medium-fast basically requires LOS, without exception. Not sure why SF deviated from the rest of Florida, which are all on long-fast. I can hear and communicate with other long-fast nodes, validated with actual communication and trace routes. MC on the other hand i can only hear repeaters, which is basically useless since i can't hear any other clients to communicate with. seems like both MT and MC are not ready as an emergency communication method during natural disasters. so I have to live on an isolated long-fast island in SF. great idea, but not very practical at this stage IMHO. i suspect those claiming both MT and MC are the greatest thing since sliced bread have a vested interest in promoting such technologies. sorry to be so cynical, but as the old saying goes, "if you want to find the truth in anything, follow the $". (edited)
messages.content_html: so far MT has been a huge dissapointment for me. Everyone is using medium-fast in SF (which is not the recommended default and deviates from the rest of Florida), which doesn't work for me becase i am in the first fresnel zone of another building 100 feet away, even with an outdoor solar node at 45' elevation. i can hear 250+ medium-fast nodes but can't communicate with any of them unless i am on top of an intercostal bridge or the jupiter lighthouse. medium-fast basically requires LOS, without exception. Not sure why SF deviated from the rest of Florida, which are all on long-fast. I can hear and communicate with other long-fast nodes, validated with actual communication and trace routes. MC on the other hand i can only hear repeaters, which is basically useless since i can't hear any other clients to communicate with. seems like both MT and MC are not ready as an emergency communication method during natural disasters. so I have to live on an isolated long-fast island in SF. great idea, but not very practical at this stage IMHO. i suspect those claiming both MT and MC are the greatest thing since sliced bread have a vested interest in promoting such technologies. sorry to be so cynical, but as the old saying goes, "if you want to find the truth in anything, follow the $". (edited)

messages.author: FapFlop
messages.text: We’re all pioneers here. It will only be “ready” for emergencies when either protocol is fully established. Florida as a whole is way behind on that when compared some other regions. Being flat sucks.
messages.content_html: We’re all pioneers here. It will only be “ready” for emergencies when either protocol is fully established. Florida as a whole is way behind on that when compared some other regions. Being flat sucks.

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: Keep in mind MC will NOT auto advert Companion nodes. Only Repeaters and Room Servers auto advert. If you want to see if you can reach someone, go on the public channel and send a message. You can also wardrive to see the current coverage in your area. Jupiter just got an MC Repeater put up a few weeks ago.
messages.content_html: Keep in mind MC will NOT auto advert Companion nodes. Only Repeaters and Room Servers auto advert. If you want to see if you can reach someone, go on the public channel and send a message. You can also wardrive to see the current coverage in your area. Jupiter just got an MC Repeater put up a few weeks ago.

messages.author: digitalrewards
messages.text: Hi to everyone I am new to mesh network but I am very interested as I am a privacy advocate. I would like some guidance on getting started and where to start .
messages.content_html: Hi to everyone I am new to mesh network but I am very interested as I am a privacy advocate. I would like some guidance on getting started and where to start .

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: Welcome. First thing I recommend is two nodes, a repeater node outside and a companion nodes for you to communicate. The repeater will help expand your reach and coverage. The companion is the node used to message via the app.
messages.content_html: Welcome. First thing I recommend is two nodes, a repeater node outside and a companion nodes for you to communicate. The repeater will help expand your reach and coverage. The companion is the node used to message via the app.

TF-Mambo
Welcome. First thing I recommend is two nodes, a repeater node outside and a companion nodes for you to communicate. The repeater will help expand your reach and coverage. The companion is the node used to message via the app.

embeds_html:
WisMesh Tag from RAKwireless & MOKOSmart - Meshtastic compatible card
What is WisMesh Tag? The WisMesh Tag is your compact companion for location tracking and mesh messaging. Built for Meshtastic users who want something portable, reliable, and ready-to-go. Unlike DIY builds or limited designs like the T1000E, the WisMesh Tag delivers reliable outdoor performance with a 1000mAh battery,

Heltec Development Board
Specifications Master Chip: ESP32-S3R2 LoRa Chip: SX-1262 Frequency: 863~928 MHZ Max. TX Power: 21±1dBm, 27±1 dBm Max. Receiving Sensitivity: -137 dBm Wi-Fi: 802.11 b/g/n, up to 150Mbps Bluetooth: Bluetooth LE, Bluetooth 5, Bluetooth mesh OLED: SSD1315 (0.96Inch, 128*64 resolution) Power Supply: ...

Wio Tracker Pro
The Wio Tracker L1 Series is a low-power, scalable Meshtastic node featuring LoRa (862–930 MHz), an nRF52840 for efficient processing, and an L76K GPS for precise location tracking. The L1 Pro version is a fully integrated, ready-to-deploy solution featuring an OLED display, rechargeable battery,...
